symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic sacral pressure area with acute 24-hour deterioration
symptoms.onset: two weeks ago, acute worsening in last 24h

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid change in a chronic pressure wound in a bedbound patient is the cardinal history for acute wound infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Foul-smelling purulent exudate from the wound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Purulent malodorous exudate signals established bacterial infection, including anaerobes.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: systemic sepsis secondary to soft-tissue infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A source of infection that can seed the bloodstream in a frail host.

symptoms.symptom_name: Peri-wound erythema, induration and warmth extending ~3 cm from the wound edge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Spreading erythema/warmth/induration is the clinical signature of cellulitis — its true extent and induration require in-person palpation and inspection the AI cannot do.
The pattern and rate of spread and any crepitus/blistering must be assessed in person to exclude a necrotising process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Necrotic black eschar and yellow slough in the wound bed with visible subcutaneous fat (full-thickness loss)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Full-thickness loss with necrotic tissue defines Stage 3 and mandates debridement — staging requires in-person inspection; the observable video appearance is suggestive but not a stage.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe new burning pain with intact sensation at the site

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Escalating inflammatory pain accompanies acute wound infection; a marked change warrants in-person analgesia review.
Intact painful sensation over a pressure-bearing sacrum argues against an insensate neuropathic aetiology.
Pain out of proportion is a warning feature that must trigger urgent in-person reassessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: No high fever, no rigors, no acute confusion; temperature only 37.6, BP/HR/RR/SpO2 stable

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: systemic sepsis secondary to soft-tissue infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of overt SIRS lowers the immediate probability of established sepsis — but only weakly, because a frail elder's febrile/haemodynamic response is blunted.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged immobility, bedbound, nursed on a standard (non-pressure-relieving) foam mattress

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sustained pressure without offloading is the mechanical cause; correcting it is foundational to any management plan.
A negative probe-to-bone lowers but does not exclude osteomyelitis; it is a hands-on test the AI cannot perform remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Wound swab microscopy, culture and sensitivities

investigation_details.investigation_name: Deep wound swab / tissue culture and sensitivities
investigation_details.expected_result: Pathogenic bacterial growth (anticipated) directing antibiotic therapy
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Empirical therapy is unguided and resistant or anaerobic organisms may be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the infecting organism and guides antibiotics — a hospital/in-person investigation never available in real time over telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person wound assessment with staging and deep wound swab (culture & sensitivities)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Needed to stage the wound, confirm and characterise the infection, and direct antibiotics — none achievable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods (FBE, CRP, U&E/creatinine, +/- blood cultures) at in-person review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess systemic infection/sepsis and renal function before antibiotic dosing in a frail elder.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Imaging for osteomyelitis (X-ray/MRI) if deep infection suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Deep chronic wound over bone with negative probe-to-bone still carries osteomyelitis risk requiring imaging.
